rationale: Title Y is more consistent with JACC clinical research title style: formal, specific, and built around disease, intervention, outcome, trial design, and follow-up. It clearly states catheter ablation for atrial fibrillation, cognitive dysfunction, reversibility, randomized design, and 12-month outcomes. Title X is punchier, but the question format, awkward '?:' punctuation, undefined AF abbreviation, and alarmist 'harm cognition' phrasing reduce editorial fit and clarity.
loser_weakness: Title X is too rhetorical and imprecise for JACC, and its punctuation and wording make it feel less like a polished clinical trial title.
